title: MFA (Multi-Factor Authentication) Setup and Troubleshooting
text:
Q: How do I set up and troubleshoot Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)?
A: To set up Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), start by logging into your company account. Windows: Open your web browser and navigate to the company login page. After entering your credentials, you will be prompted to set up MFA. Choose your preferred method (e.g., authentication app, SMS, email). Follow the on-screen instructions to link your device. macOS: Similarly, open your web browser and go to the company login page. Enter your credentials, and when prompted, select your MFA method and follow the instructions to complete the setup. If you encounter issues, ensure your device's time settings are correct, as this can affect authentication apps. Windows: If you're using an authentication app and it fails, try re-syncing the app or reinstalling it. macOS: For the same issue, check if the app is updated to the latest version. If you lose access to your MFA method, contact IT support immediately for recovery options. Always keep backup codes in a secure location for emergencies.
